## Summary

Aligns the Pass 1 and Pass 2 model routing cascade in `evaluationRuntimeConfig.ts` to match `policy.ts`. The runtime config was missing `EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL` from its fallback chain, so setting it in Vercel had no effect on the diagnostic routing snapshot — all calls fell through to the default `gpt-5.1`.

**Before (broken):**
```
Pass 1: EVAL_PASS1_MODEL → EVAL_CHUNK_MODEL → default (gpt-5.1)
Pass 2: EVAL_PASS2_MODEL → EVAL_CHUNK_MODEL → default (gpt-5.1)
```

**After (fixed):**
```
Pass 1: EVAL_PASS1_MODEL → EVAL_CHUNK_MODEL → EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL → default (gpt-5.1)
Pass 2: EVAL_PASS2_MODEL → EVAL_CHUNK_MODEL → EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL → default (gpt-5.1)
Pass 3: EVAL_PASS3_MODEL → EVAL_SYNTHESIS_MODEL → default (gpt-5.1) [unchanged]
```

**After merging, add this Vercel env var:**

| Variable | Value | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL` | `gpt-4o-mini` | Routes high-volume phases (Pass 1/2 chunks, seeds, ledger, polish) to cheap model |

This alone should reduce evaluation costs by ~70-80% since the bulk of API calls are chunk evaluations.

## Scope

- `lib/config/evaluationRuntimeConfig.ts` — added `EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL` to Pass 1 and Pass 2 routing cascades

Does NOT touch: policy.ts (already correct), pipeline logic, Pass 3 routing, scoring, UI.

## Branch Freshness (Never Behind)

Branch-Behind-Base: 0

## Risks & Anomalies

- `gpt-4o-mini` output quality is lower than `gpt-5.1`. If Pass 1/2 chunk evaluations need the stronger model for accuracy, the user can remove `EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL` or set `EVAL_CHUNK_MODEL=gpt-5.1` to override.
- Pass 3 synthesis intentionally stays on `gpt-5.1` — this is the author-facing output where quality matters most.
- Seeds, ledger, and polish already respected `EVAL_CHEAP_MODEL` via `policy.ts`; this fix ensures the diagnostic routing agrees.

---

No-Pipeline-Impact: N/A — this IS a pipeline behavior change (activates cheap model routing for Pass 1/2 when env var is set).

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/f13ac2ba44eb420f993f365f7600a0b5
